title: tecticornia pruinosa
intro: Tecticornia pruinosa, also known as the white glasswort, is a succulent plant that belongs to the family Halophytaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal and health benefits. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. Tecticornia pruinosa can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, allowing for its health benefits to be harnessed for various purposes.
